name: Tiergarten
notes: Opening section out of the pits. Series of medium-speed corners through the forest.

name: Hatzenbach
notes: Series of fast S-bends through the forest. Rhythm section requiring commitment.

name: Hocheichen
notes: Fast right-hander. Name means 'high oaks'.

name: Quiddelbacher Höhe
notes: High-speed crest section. Significant elevation change.

name: Flugplatz
notes: Name means 'airfield'. Famous for cars going airborne over the crest. One of the most iconic sections.

name: Schwedenkreuz
notes: Very fast left-hander. Name means 'Swedish Cross'. Historically dangerous section.

name: Aremberg
notes: Downhill right-hander. Named after the nearby Aremberg castle. Major braking from high speed.

name: Fuchsröhre
notes: Name means 'Fox Hollow'. Fast downhill section through a valley. Cars get airborne at the bottom.

name: Adenauer Forst
notes: Technical section through Adenau forest. Multiple direction changes. Named after the nearby town of Adenau.

name: Metzgesfeld
notes: Series of medium-speed corners.

name: Kallenhard
notes: Right-hand section, medium speed.

name: Mercedes-Arena
notes: Technical sequence of corners. Tight, demanding section.

name: Ex-Mühle
notes: Name means 'former mill'. Right-hand corner.

name: Bergwerk
notes: Name means 'mine' or 'mine works'. Slow right-hander, historically one of the most dangerous corners.

name: Kesselchen
notes: Fast section across an open plateau. Name means 'little cauldron'. Long high-speed commitment required.

name: Klostertal
notes: Name means 'monastery valley'. Fast downhill section with limited visibility.

name: Caracciola-Karussell
notes: The iconic banked hairpin. Named after Rudolf Caracciola. The concrete banking is original from the 1920s. One of the most famous corners in motorsport.

name: Hohe Acht
notes: Named after the highest peak in the region at 747m. Right-hander near the circuit's highest point.

name: Wippermann
notes: Series of corners named after a local family.

name: Eschbach
notes: Right-hand section. Named after the local Eschbach stream.

name: Brünnchen
notes: Series of two corners (Brünnchen 1 and 2). Popular spectator area. Name means 'little spring/well'.

name: Eiffelblitz
notes: Fast section. Name means 'Eifel lightning'.

name: Pflanzgarten
notes: Three-part fast section (Pflanzgarten 1, 2, 3). Name means 'plant garden'. Cars get airborne. Extremely demanding at speed.

name: Stefan Bellof S
notes: Named after Stefan Bellof who set the legendary 6:11 lap record here in 1983. Fast S-bend.

name: Schwalbenschwanz
notes: Name means 'swallowtail'. Series of right-left corners through the forest.

name: Galgenkopf
notes: Name means 'gallows head'. Right-hand corner near the end of the circuit.

name: Döttinger Höhe
notes: The long flat-out straight back towards the start. Over 2km. Cars reach 280-300km/h. Named after the nearby village of Döttingen.

name: Antoniusbusch
notes: Final section before rejoining the GP circuit. Named after a local chapel.

name: Tiergarten (end)
notes: Final corners rejoining the GP circuit start area.
